title: Programmable Logic Controller (PLC)
learning_objectives: Understand the purpose of a Programmable Logic Controller (PLC).
Identify the major components of a PLC.
Learn the applications of PLCs in industrial automation.
Recognize how PLCs improve manufacturing efficiency and reliability.

introduction.title: What is a PLC?
introduction.content: A Programmable Logic Controller (PLC) is an industrial computer designed to monitor inputs, execute control logic and control outputs in real time. PLCs replace traditional relay-based control systems and are widely used for automating industrial processes due to their reliability, flexibility and ease of programming.

business_purpose.title: Why are PLCs Important?
business_purpose.content: PLCs automate repetitive industrial tasks, improve production consistency, reduce human error and increase equipment reliability. They enable industries like JSL to achieve continuous production with minimal downtime while ensuring safe and efficient machine operation.

plc_components.purpose: Executes the PLC program and processes input/output data.
Provides electrical power to the PLC system.
Receives signals from sensors, switches and field devices.
Sends control signals to actuators, motors, valves and relays.
Used to create, edit and download PLC programs.
Enables communication with SCADA, HMIs and other PLCs.

plc_scan_cycle: Read input signals from field devices.

programming_languages.description: Most commonly used graphical programming language for PLCs.
Uses interconnected function blocks to create logic.
High-level programming language similar to Pascal.

limitations: Higher initial investment compared to relay systems.
Requires trained personnel for programming and maintenance.
workflow: Input signal is sent to the PLC.
Output signal activates the required device.
The process continues until new input conditions are detected.

integration.purpose: SCADA monitors and supervises PLC-controlled equipment.
Operators interact with PLC-controlled machines through HMI screens.
Provide real-time input data to the PLC.
Execute commands issued by the PLC.
Production and machine data can be transferred to enterprise systems for reporting and analysis.

business_example.title: PLC in JSL
business_example.content: In a stainless steel rolling mill, sensors continuously monitor strip thickness, motor speed and temperature. The PLC processes these inputs and automatically adjusts rolling speed, motor operation and cooling systems to maintain product quality and production efficiency.

best_practices: Protect PLC cabinets from dust and moisture.
Use secure passwords for PLC programming software.
common_errors: Failure to back up PLC programs.
important_notes: PLCs operate continuously and perform thousands of scan cycles every minute.
Modern PLCs can communicate with SCADA, HMIs and cloud platforms.
PLCs are designed to withstand harsh industrial environments.
Proper preventive maintenance improves PLC reliability and lifespan.
